title: Searching Algorithms Coding
description: Coding challenges implementing linear search and binary search from scratch.
subject: Programming

content.prompt: Read a list of space-separated integers, then read a target integer. Implement linear search — check each element one by one. If found, print 'Found at index <i>'. If not found, print 'Not found'.
content.starterCode:
# TODO: Read list and target
nums = list(map(int, input("Enter numbers: ").split()))
target = int(input("Enter target: "))

# TODO: Implement linear search
# Loop through nums, compare each to target
# Print 'Found at index <i>' or 'Not found'

content.prompt: Read a sorted list of space-separated integers, then read a target. Implement binary search using left and right pointers. Print 'Found at index <i>' if found, or 'Not found'. Also print the number of comparisons made.
content.starterCode:
# TODO: Read sorted list and target
nums = list(map(int, input("Enter sorted numbers: ").split()))
target = int(input("Enter target: "))

# TODO: Implement binary search
# left = 0, right = len(nums) - 1
# While left <= right:
#   mid = (left + right) // 2
#   Compare nums[mid] with target
#   Count each comparison
# Print 'Found at index <i>' or 'Not found'
# Print 'Comparisons: <count>'

testCases.description: Target 7 found at mid on first try
